name: Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario
name_original: Peña de la Cruz – circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario
region_intro: Hiking around Béjar offers diverse terrain, from green forests to steep mountains, within the southeast of Salamanca province. The region is characterized by its varied ecosystems, including well-conserved oak, chestnut, and ash forests, and rivers fed by mountain snowmelt. Altitudes range significantly, with peaks reaching up to 2,425 meters, providing demanding ascents and extensive views. Much of the area is part of the Sierras de Béjar y Francia Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-designated protected space. Best hiking trails around Béjar The most popular hiking route is Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the natural environment of the Sierra de Candelario. Another top favourite among local hikers is Béjar - El Castañar Trail, a difficult 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This route leads to the historic Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Castañar. Local hikers also love the Fuente de la Hoja – Ermita del Humilladero loop from Béjar, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Béjar is defined by diverse forests, mountainous terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Béjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Béjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Béjar?
A: Béjar offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 40 routes documented on komoot. These trails cater to all levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents, exploring the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in the Béjar area?
A: The terrain around Béjar is incredibly diverse, ranging from lush, green forests of oak, chestnut, and ash to steep mountain paths with peaks reaching up to 2,425 meters. You'll also find trails alongside rivers like the Cuerpo de Hombre, and through varied ecosystems influenced by both Mediterranean and Eurosiberian climates.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Béjar?
A: Yes, Béjar has several easy routes suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed here, the region offers many gentle paths through its beautiful forests and natural areas that are perfect for a leisurely walk with children. Look for routes marked as 'easy' on komoot for suitable options.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Béjar?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Béjar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993358755/pena-de-la-cruz-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-candelario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10371657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Hoja – Ermita del Humilladero loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate trail.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking near Béjar?
A: The Béjar area is rich in natural and historical sights. You can hike to the historic Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Castañar via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993374061/bejar-el-castanar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar - El Castañar Trail&lt;/a&gt;. For stunning views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón (Calvitero Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, offering vistas from 2,400 meters. The historic Vía de la Plata also runs through the area, featuring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar Station and Vía de la Plata Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery on the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Béjar offers numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5304498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – Cedro de la Francesa loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route known for its scenic outlooks. For more challenging hikes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1275875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossroads and beautiful views – Views on the embalse de Navamuño loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views, including those of the Navamuño Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Béjar?
A: Béjar offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year due to its varied ecosystems. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, but higher mountain trails offer cooler conditions. Winter hiking is possible, particularly at lower elevations, but higher peaks may require snow gear, especially near the La Covatilla ski resort.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Béjar?
A: The hiking trails in Béjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from dense forests to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained network of paths that cater to various fitness levels. The stunning viewpoints and the rich natural environment of the Sierras de Béjar y Francia Biosphere Reserve are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Béjar?
A: Béjar itself offers various amenities, and some routes may pass close to establishments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/987118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pequeña Estación Bar Restaurant Café&lt;/a&gt; is an option near some trails. It's always a good idea to check local listings or plan your route to pass through towns or villages where you can find cafes and restaurants.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Béjar?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Béjar, especially at common starting points for trails. Many routes begin near the town center or at designated trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for information on parking facilities closest to your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking opportunities in the Béjar region?
A: Yes, the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road, passes through the Béjar area and offers an impressive long-distance hiking and cycling route. This allows visitors to explore diverse landscapes and historical sites over extended distances, connecting various towns and natural areas.
tips:
The Peña de la Cruz shelter has 20 beds and offers traditional cuisine for both guests and passers-by. 

According to its own promotion, it is an ideal place to go and see the stars with friends.

You can ascend from Béjar along the road, making stops at the Sagrado Corazón and Fuente del Lobo, to reach El Castañar.
At the end of the path we find a great esplanade with picnic areas and fountains that is presided over by a huge granite cross and its viewpoint located on a rock.

waypoints.name: Peña de la Cruz and Balanced Boulder
